qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code Node VM2 Sandbox Escape permits arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes VM2 sandbox escape in the Code node (JavaScriptSandbox.ts, Code.node.ts). The advisory describes expression sandbox escape in the expression evaluation system ({{ }} template expressions in workflow parameters). These are different components with different root causes: Code node VM2 sandboxing vs expression evaluation sandboxing.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ExecuteCommand Node permits arbitrary shell command execution without sandboxing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes the ExecuteCommand node running arbitrary shell commands via Node.js exec(). The advisory describes expression sandbox escape in expression evaluation. The ExecuteCommand node is designed to execute commands and is not a sandbox escape; the root cause is the absence of node-type restrictions, not a sandbox bypass.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in workflow import from URL allows internal network reconnaissance
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via the workflow import URL endpoint (axios.get without URL validation). The advisory describes expression sandbox escape in expression evaluation. Different component, different vulnerability class, different root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated webhook execution allows unauthorized workflow triggering
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes webhook endpoints executing workflows without authentication by default. The advisory describes expression sandbox escape. Different component (webhook authentication vs expression evaluation), different vulnerability class, different root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-vpcf-gvg4-6qwr) describes an expression sandbox escape in n8n's expression evaluation system, where crafted {{ }} expressions in workflow parameters escape the sandbox and trigger system command execution. None of the four qualifying findings identify this root cause. The code_node_vm2_sandbox_escape finding describes VM2 sandbox escape in the Code node (a different component from expression evaluation). The execute_command_rce finding describes the ExecuteCommand node running shell commands by design (not a sandbox escape). The ssrf_workflow_import and unauthenticated_webhook_execution findings target entirely different vulnerability classes.
evidence: Advisory title: 'n8n: Expression Sandbox Escape Leads to RCE' — refers to expression evaluation, not Code node VM2, ExecuteCommand, webhook auth, or SSRF
Advisory references patch commits 1479aab2d32fe0ee087f82b9038b1035c98be2f6 and 9e5212ecbc5d2d4e6f340b636a5e84be6369882e for expression evaluation fixes
Advisory follows CVE-2025-68613 (GHSA-v98v-ff95-f3cp), also an expression sandbox escape, confirming the root cause is in the expression evaluation system
code_node_vm2_sandbox_escape targets Code.node.ts and JavaScriptSandbox.ts — the Code node's VM2 sandbox, not the expression evaluation sandbox
execute_command_rce targets ExecuteCommand.node.ts — a node designed to run commands, not a sandbox escape
No finding references the expression evaluation engine, expression parser, or {{ }} expression rendering pipeline
